description_long: This bungalow is a single-story log structure with a partially constructed roof, revealing exposed wooden rafters and beams on one side. The exterior walls are made of horizontally stacked logs, giving it a rustic, cabin-like appearance. The roof is a combination of solid brown shingles and open framework, with a prominent cross-gabled design. A small covered porch with a peaked roof and wooden steps marks the main entrance at the front. Large rectangular windows are present on the front and sides, providing ample natural light. The overall design suggests a blend of traditional craftsmanship and modern residential comfort.
description: A rustic log bungalow with a cross-gabled roof, partially exposed rafters, and a covered front porch. Features large windows and a mix of finished and unfinished roofing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a covered porch, large windows, and partially finished roof structure.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the cross-gabled roof and the log wall with no visible windows or doors.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the exposed wooden rafters and beams, as well as the overall roof structure.
description_view_3: Side view displaying the log wall, a single window, and the extension of the cross-gabled roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large front and side windows
